items.name: Podman
items.summary: Container tool update; commenters praised easy Docker Desktop migrations and rootless workflows.
items.why_included: Strong adoption signal from people actually switching to Podman.
items.evidence: Several commenters said moving from Docker Desktop was easy and that Podman was a better implementation, with zero-compose changes and less daemon overhead.

items.name: PeerTube
items.source_story_title: PeerTube is a free, decentralized and federated video platform
items.summary: Federated video platform for running your own video site.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as a realistic own-your-distribution alternative to YouTube.
items.evidence: Replies pointed to KDE and Blender instances, and argued that owning your own domain and hosting channel is the point.

items.name: Manufact
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Manufact (YC S25) – MCP Cloud
items.summary: MCP cloud platform for deploying and monetizing MCP servers.
items.why_included: Launch thread had concrete questions about marketplace distribution and monetization.
items.evidence: Commenters asked about OpenAI and Claude store apps plus monetization, and the founders answered with pricing and credits details.

items.name: slopo
items.source_story_title: Show HN: CLI tool for detecting non-exact code duplication with embedding models
items.summary: Embedding-based code duplication detector for non-exact clones.
items.why_included: Solid utility with clear use case and immediate technical feedback.
items.evidence: The author said it finds similar-looking code with embeddings, and commenters asked about function granularity, dependencies, and BM25 baselines.

items.name: SailfishOS
items.source_story_title: Android Developer Verification: Threat masquerading as protection
items.summary: Linux mobile OS that commenters cited as a real Android alternative.
items.why_included: Positive mobile-Linux alternative surfaced from a large security-policy thread.
items.evidence: Commenters called out SailfishOS as one of the actual Linux OSes for mobile, and another said they liked using it.

items.name: Librem 5
items.source_story_title: Android Developer Verification: Threat masquerading as protection
items.summary: Privacy-focused Linux phone that one commenter uses as their daily handset.
items.why_included: Concrete hardware recommendation from the same mobile-platform discussion.
items.evidence: A commenter said they use a Librem 5 daily, noted PureOS is actively developed, and tied it to Waydroid/PureOS.

items.name: EXAPUNKS
items.summary: Programming puzzle game that still gets love from Zachtronics fans.
items.why_included: Classic game with explicit praise and companion recommendations.
items.evidence: Fans called it an all-time favorite and compared it with Shenzhen I/O, TIS-100, and SpaceChem.

items.name: U.V.S. Nirmana
items.summary: Engineering puzzle game from Coincidence, the new Zachtronics-adjacent release.
items.why_included: Fresh concrete follow-on recommendation from the Exapunks thread.
items.evidence: A commenter said Coincidence had just come out with a spacecraft engineering puzzle game and linked U.V.S. Nirmana.

items.name: FreeGraphPaper
items.source_story_title: Show HN: A graph paper generator that renders vector PDFs in the browser
items.summary: Browser-based graph paper generator that outputs true-to-scale PDFs.
items.why_included: Simple utility with clear user demand for export and extra grid types.
items.evidence: Commenters asked for SVG export, hex grids, plotter support, and praised the tool’s usefulness and polish.

telegram_footer: 9 items surfaced | 23/23 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated
